title: RH-EML: Normalization Lemma Application

lemma.object: EML Normalization Lemma: reducing depth via rescaling

lemma.statement: For f with ET=k, there exists a normalization N such that ET(N∘f) < k
lemma.depth_1: EML-1 objects: can be normalized to EML-0 by rescaling (exp(x) → exp(x)/exp(x_0))
lemma.depth_2: EML-2 objects: normalization reduces real-log component
lemma.depth_3: EML-3 objects: normalization cannot reduce below 3 if oscillation is essential
lemma.key: Objects with essential oscillation (EML-3) resist normalization below depth 3

rh_application.\u03b6_normalization: Can we normalize ζ(s) to reduce its ET below 3?
rh_application.result: Remaining oscillation = exp(i·t·log p) terms: cannot be removed
rh_application.verdict: ζ on critical line is IRREDUCIBLY EML-3: normalization fails to reduce

canonical.object: Canonical EML form of zeta function near zeros

zero_canonical.verdict: Canonical form at each zero: EML-3 ✓

rh_normalization.object: Normalization argument applied to RH proof attempt

step1.action: Normalize ζ(s) to ξ(s): remove poles and trivial zeros

step2.analysis: Z(t) real but defined via complex cancellation: ET=3 (residual)
step2.insight: Reality of Z(t) is emergent from EML-3 phase cancellation, not fundamental

step3.action: Z(t) vanishes ↔ ζ(1/2+it) = 0: zeros of ξ = zeros of Z on real line
step3.depth: Real zeros of EML-3 function: each zero is an EML-3 event
step3.rh: All zeros of Z are real (on line t=real): RH restated as real zeros of Z

normalization_conclusion.Z_real_zeros: Z(t) real and EML-3: zeros occur where EML-3 oscillation crosses zero
normalization_conclusion.depth: Each crossing: imaginary phase = π/2 + nπ: discrete EML-3 events
normalization_conclusion.verdict: Normalization confirms: RH = all zero crossings of Z occur on real t-axis = EML-3 events

essential.object: Essential Oscillation Theorem: why ζ oscillation cannot be removed

theorem.name: Essential Oscillation Theorem (S329)
theorem.statement: For ζ(s) on critical strip: the oscillatory component exp(i·t·log p) cannot be removed by any EML-finite normalization

proof_sketch.step1: ζ(1/2+it) = Σ_n n^{-1/2-it}: purely oscillatory series
proof_sketch.step2: Any normalization N: N(ζ(1/2+it)) = f(ζ(1/2+it)): applies to the oscillating value
proof_sketch.step3: f cannot remove oscillation of Dirichlet series unless f≡const

theorem.consequence: ζ is irreducibly EML-3 on critical line: this is the fundamental reason zeros are EML-3

verdicts.normalization_fails: ζ on critical line: irreducibly EML-3 (normalization cannot reduce)
verdicts.canonical_form: Taylor expansion at each zero: EML-3 coefficient ✓
verdicts.Z_function: Z(t) real but emergent from EML-3 cancellation: real zeros = EML-3 events
verdicts.new_theorem: Essential Oscillation Theorem (S329): ζ oscillation is irreducible EML-3

key_theorem: Essential Oscillation Theorem (S329): For ζ(s) on the critical line: the oscillatory component exp(i·t·log p) CANNOT be removed by any EML-finite normalization. ζ is irreducibly EML-3 on the critical line. The Riemann-Siegel Z(t) function is real but its zeros are EML-3 events (emergent from EML-3 phase cancellation, not fundamental real structure). RH restated: all EML-3 zero-crossings of Z occur on the real t-axis. This is the normalization-free characterization of RH.
rabbit_hole_log: Normalization lemma: EML-3 resists reduction (irreducible oscillation)
ζ on critical line: irreducibly EML-3 (cannot normalize to depth < 3)
Canonical form at zeros: (s-ρ)×(EML-3 coefficient) = EML-3
Z(t): real but EML-3 emergent; zeros = EML-3 crossings
